title: Week of 22 June
intro: A vegan core every night that works for both of you, with simple add-ons for the kids and a dairy finish for you. Familiar, kid-safe, and built around what's in season.

days.dish: Coconut red-lentil dal, rice & green beans
days.core: Simmer red lentils with onion, garlic, ginger, turmeric and cumin in coconut milk until creamy. Serve over basmati with green beans sautéed in a little oil. Finish with lemon and coriander.
days.kids: Boiled egg halves or oven fish fingers
days.dairy: A spoon of plain yoghurt stirred in
days.toddler: Mash dal into the rice, cut beans into short pieces, hold the salt.

days.core: Roast diced zucchini in olive oil, then stir into a simple garlic–tomato sauce with basil. Toss through penne.
days.kids: Pan-cooked Wurst slices on the side
days.toddler: Use small pasta shapes or cut the penne; the sauce is naturally soft.

days.dish: Herby traybake: new potatoes, fennel, carrots & chickpeas
days.core: Toss new potatoes, fennel wedges, carrots and a tin of chickpeas with olive oil, rosemary and thyme. Roast about 35 minutes until golden.
days.kids: A baked salmon or white fish fillet alongside
days.dairy: Yoghurt mixed with dill and a little lemon as a dip
days.toddler: Soft potato and carrot cut small; skip the crispy edges.

days.dish: Lentil & vegetable soup with crusty bread
days.core: Brown lentils simmered with onion, carrot, celery, tomato and a bay leaf into a hearty soup. Serve with good crusty bread.
days.kids: Sliced Wurst dropped into their bowl
days.toddler: Blend a portion smooth, low salt, serve warm not hot.

days.core: Wilt fresh spinach into a tomato-onion masala with cumin, coriander and garam masala, with chickpeas. Serve with rice.
Flatbreads topped with tomato sauce, thin zucchini, peppers and olives, baked hot. Fun to let the four-year-old help assemble.
days.kids: Ham or tuna topping on theirs
days.toddler: Cut into small soft squares, go easy on toppings.

days.core: Mixed summer veg (carrot, peas, green beans, potato) simmered in a mild cashew–coconut sauce. Big batch — leftovers cover Monday's lunch. Serve with rice.
days.kids: Baked fish or a boiled egg
days.dairy: A swirl of cream or yoghurt

breakfasts: Porridge with fruit and a little nut butter
Toast with jam or nut butter; fruit on the side
Yoghurt (soy for the partner) with strawberries
Weekend: idli or dosa as a treat
lunches: Leftover dal or korma with rice
Veggie sticks with hummus and bread
Simple sandwiches with cucumber and cheese / hummus

items: Ham slices or tinned tuna — small pack/tin

preptip: On Sunday, make the vegetable korma as a big batch — it reheats well and covers Monday's lunch. While the oven is on for Wednesday's traybake, roast extra veg to fold into Thursday's soup or Saturday's flatbreads. Wash and prep the beans, fennel and carrots early so weeknights are mostly assembly.
